LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MEDIVATION, INC., AND MEDIVATION PROSTATE THERAPEUTICS, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE APPEAL OF THE STATEMENT OF DECISION IN MEDIVATION ET AL. V. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, ET AL. (SF SUPERIOR COURT CASE NO. CGC-11-510715) ("MEDIVATION I") WARRANTS STAYING THIS DISTINCT ACTION. ALTHOUGH BOTH CASES INVOLVE THE SAME CONTRACT AND THE SAME PARTIES, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE OUTCOME OF THE APPEAL WILL SUBSTANTIALLY AFFECT THIS CASE. THE STATEMENT OF DECISION IN MEDIVATION I CONSIDERS THE NARROW ISSUE OF WHETHER SALES MILESTONE PAYMENTS ARE "SUBLICENSING INCOME," AS DEFINED IN THE EXCLUSIVE LICENSE AGREEMENT ("ELA"). THE STATEMENT OF DECISION DID NOT BROADLY DEFINE "SUBLICENSING INCOME." BECAUSE THE NARROW ISSUE ON APPEAL IS WHETHER SALES MILESTONE PAYMENTS ARE "SUBLICENSING INCOME," THE QUESTION PRESENTED IN THIS CASE, WHETHER PROFIT-SHARING REVENUE SHOULD BE CONSIDERED "SUBLICENSING INCOME" UNDER THE ELA, SHOULD BE UNAFFECTED BY THE OUTCOME OF THE APPEAL. A STAY IS NOT NECESSARY TO ENSURE CONSISTENCY BETWEEN LEGAL RULINGS IN THE TWO CASES. FURTHER, A STAY WILL NOT PROMOTE JUDICIAL ECONOMY. THE PARTIES CAN WORK TOGETHER TO AVOID DUPLICATIVE DISCOVERY FROM MEDIVATION I, TO THE EXTENT OVERLAP EXISTS. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS MEDIVATION'S ARGUMENTS FOR STAY BASED UPON BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIMS IN THE CURRENT CASE THAT HAVE NOW BEEN DISMISSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Medivation, Inc. And Medivation Prostate Therapeutics, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NATHANIEL P. BUALAT OF CROWELL & MORING, TEL. NO. 986-2800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES K. LYNCH OF LATHAM & WATKINS LLP, TEL. NO. 395-8265 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MEDIVATION, INC., and MEDIVATION PROSTATE THERAPEUTICS, INC.'s Motion To Stay is Denied. Defendants have not made a sufficient showing that the appeal of the Statement of Decision in Medivation et al. v. The Regents of the University of California, et al. (SF Superior Court case no. CGC-11-510715) ("Medivation I") warrants staying this distinct action. Although both cases involve the same contract and the same parties, Defendants have not established that the outcome of the appeal will substantially affect this case. The Statement of Decision in Medivation I considers the narrow issue of whether sales milestone payments are "Sublicensing Income," as defined in the Exclusive License Agreement ("ELA"). The Statement of Decision did not broadly define "Sublicensing Income." Because the narrow issue on appeal is whether sales milestone payments are "Sublicensing Income," the question presented in this case, whether profit-sharing revenue should be considered "Sublicensing Income" under the ELA, should be unaffected by the outcome of the appeal. A stay is not necessary to ensure consistency between legal rulings in the two cases. Further, a stay will not promote judicial economy. The parties can work together to avoid duplicative discovery from Medivation I, to the extent overlap exists. The Court does not address Medivation's arguments for stay based upon breach of fiduciary duty claims in the current case that have now been dismissed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM
